original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days the base case is a range-bound to corrective tape: a failed recovery rally that stalled at the 23.6% fib / swing-high resistance band and rolled over, with downside-first probability dominant and breadth/credit still refusing to confirm. Expect a retest of the 7,392 caution / 7,240 nervous zone unless price can decisively reclaim and hold above 7,500-7,553 with breadth improvement.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day (~7,301) above 200-day (~6,897) and still rising — mechanically intact bullish regime, but price has rolled back below the 23.6% fib and sits only ~1.6% above the 50-day after a sharp distribution day. Trend skeleton bullish, momentum decisively negative.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE ~20.05x (moderate buying at support band); ERP ~0.52% sits right at the 0.5% valuation-ceiling floor — thin-to-zero cushion, trim bias, no aggressive adds.

signals.vix_regime: Standard operating regime: VIX ~18.4, below the 20 risk threshold but ticking up toward its declining 50-day MA — no capitulation spike present.
signals.bollinger_position: Rejected at upper/middle band and rolling back toward the 20-day SMA after the recovery failed near the 23.6% fib.
signals.middle_band_regime: Price falling back toward the 20-day SMA / middle band after the relief rally stalled below the swing high.
signals.intraday_close_position: Bottom third of range — weak, distribution-style close near 7,420 after a steady all-day sell from ~7,530.

original_tool_input.what_changed_today: RECOVERY FAILED / ROLLOVER: After reclaiming the 23.6% fib (~7,553) on 6/15, price was rejected at the swing-high resistance band and sold off hard — close 7,420.10 (-~1.8% intraday from morning highs near 7,530), back below the 23.6% fib and below the 38.2%/50%/61.8% fibs.
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: The early-June recovery rally stalled exactly where the framework warned it might — at the 23.6% fib / swing-high resistance band — and reversed in a textbook distribution session, closing near the lows at 7,420 after fading all day from ~7,530. The bull skeleton remains mechanically intact (50d > 200d, price ~7.6% above the 200-day), but ERP is pinned at the 0.5% valuation ceiling, breadth (McClellan Extreme Fear) and credit (junk spread ~1.35%) still refuse to confirm, and the Monte Carlo edge is decisively down-first (~74%, 2-day median to downside). This is a failed recovery into resistance inside a Late Bull / Topping regime — defensive patience over chasing.
open_questions: Does price hold the 7,392 caution / rising 50-day SMA (~7,301) support shelf, or does the rollover extend into the 7,240 nervous zone?
Will breadth (McClellan) and credit (junk spread) ever confirm a price recovery, or does their persistent divergence confirm the rally was narrow and the top is in?
Can the bulls reclaim 7,500-7,553 with momentum, or is the 23.6%/swing-high band now confirmed overhead resistance?

rows.signal: Neutral-to-weak; momentum rolling over, no buy signal

Defensive patience — trim into any bounce, no adds; let support and breadth confirm before re-entry

confirming_evidence: Recovery rally stalled precisely at the 23.6% fib / swing-high resistance band and reversed — failed extension confirms the topping thesis.
Distribution-style session: all-day fade from ~7,530 to a bottom-third close at 7,420.
ERP ~0.52% sits at the 0.5% valuation ceiling — no cushion, trim bias.
Monte Carlo edge decisively down-first (~74%) with a 2-day median to the downside target; well below all action thresholds.
RSI-14 ~41 and MFI ~38 rolling over — momentum deteriorating, no buy trigger.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: 50d > 200d and price ~7.6% above the 200-day keeps the long-term bull trend mechanically intact.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: McClellan Extreme Fear and junk spreads wide (~1.35%) refuse to confirm price — narrow leadership and credit warning while the index sits near resistance.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not yet broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Forward PE ~20x is only moderately rich and the trend structure is still up.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP ~0.52% is pinned at the valuation ceiling — equities offer near-zero premium over the 10y, capping aggressive adds.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: ERP 0.0-0.5% = valuation ceiling, trim bias, no aggressive adds.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: VIX ~18.4 is orderly, below 20 — no panic, suggesting controlled selling rather than a crash.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: The absence of a VIX capitulation spike means no washout low has been marked, so the down-first Monte Carlo edge and rollover lack a confirmed bottom.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: A correction without a volatility spike should be treated as orderly, not capitulative; re-entry requires confirmation.

original_tool_input.primary_tension: A mechanically intact bull skeleton (50d > 200d, price ~7.6% above the 200-day) is colliding with a failed recovery rally that was rejected at the 23.6% fib / swing-high resistance band, a distribution-style rollover close, an ERP pinned at the 0.5% valuation ceiling, breadth and credit still in Extreme Fear, and a decisively down-first Monte Carlo edge — leaving defensive patience over chasing strength as the only justified posture.
